heading: Programme 16
intro: Palatalization is an important feature of the consonantal system in Kashmiri. It is a distinctive feature and has matical function particularly in singular plural distinction.

heading: Programme 17
intro: Nasalisation is a phonemic feature in Kashmiri. Vowels are Nasalized.

heading: Programme 18
intro: I. In this Programme, you will be introduced to

sections.title: Sample Dialogue
items: No that is not a shirt, that is a sweater.
Yes, this is a note book.

rows.kashmiri: na ha chanI kAmi:z, ha cha baniya:n
rows.english: No that is not a shirt, that is a sweater.
Yes, this is a note book.

heading: Programme 19
intro: I. In this Programme you will be introduced to

sections.title: Forms of the auxiliary verb a:sun ato bea
Sample Dialogue
items: They are not lady doctors. They are also nurses.
I am also a nurse. What are you?
No, I am not a teacher.
Yes, you are also an employee and I am also an employee.

rows.kashmiri: na humI chanI Da:kTarba:yi humI ti cha nArsI
rows.english: They are not lady doctors. They are also nurses.
I am also a nurse. What are you?
No, I am not a teacher.

rows.kashmiri: a: tsI ti chakh mulA:zim tI bI ti chus mulA:zim.
rows.english: Yes, you are also an employee and I am also an employee.

heading: Programme 20
intro: I. In this Programme, you will be introduced to

sections.title: Sample Dialogue
items: My mother is in the office.
Our neighbors are Hari and Ashok. This is Harias house and that is Ashokas.
No, she is not our neighbour.
This isjias picture. He is our uncle.
Our aunt is in the market.

rows.english: My mother is in the office.

rows.kashmiri: sA:na hamsa:yi chi hari tI ashokI yi chu hariyun maka:nI tI hu chu ashokun.
rows.english: Our neighbors are Hari and Ashok. This is Harias house and that is Ashokas.
No, she is not our neighbour.

rows.kashmiri: yi chu ra:mjiyun pho:T u:, su chu so:n petIr.
rows.english: This isjias picture. He is our uncle.
Our aunt is in the market.

sections.title: Listen and memorize the kinship terms

heading: Programme 21
intro: I. This Programme introduces

sections.title: Let us listen to the conversation:
items: rashi:d kati chu ? - Where is Rashid?
su chu sri:nagarI - He is at Srinagar.
tAmasund boD bo:y kati chu ? - Where is his elder brother?
su chu jemi - He is at Jammu.
tAma sInz beni kati cha ? - Where is his sister?
sa cha Dili - She is at Delhi.
yi cha: tihund ba:g ? - Is this their orchard?
a: yi chu tihund ba:g - Yes, this is their orchard.
ba:gukrakbI ko:tah chu ? - What is the area of the orchard?
yemaukrakbI chu zI kana:l - It is spread over two canals.
yim kam kam kula chi ? - Which trees are these?
yim chi tsu: Tha kula Du:na bula tse:rI kuji ,gila:s bula tI Tangkula - These are apple, walnut, apricot, cherry and pear trees.
hu thod kul kaa: chu ? - What is that tall tree?
sa cha yA:r - That is pine tree.
kaa:h sabIz cha ! - What a green it is!
tsu:Tha cha: mAdIra - Are the apples sweet?
na yim chi tsoka - No these are sour.
yim vazIla tI le dIra po:sh chi rIta - These red and yellow flowers are beautiful.

sections.title: Try to remember the colour terms

heading: Programme 22
intro: I. In this Programme we introduce

sections.title: Genitive forms
items: lA kri hInzI - of the wood

sections.title: Let us listen to the conversation about a House
items: Nasi:ma tsI chakhi: va:ray ? - Nasima, are you okay?
yeth ka:lni: kaa: chu na:v ? - What is the name of this colony?
yi cha: nAv ka:lni: ? - Is it a new colony?
a: so:n maka:nI ti chu yeti: - Yes, our house is also situated here.
ko:ta boD chu ? - How big is it?
ja:n boD, tath chi tso:r kamrI - Quite big, it has four rooms.
pash kamaukchu ? - What is roof made of?
maka:nIka darva:zI kittha chi ? - How are the doors of the house?
bADa tI mazbu:t - Big and strong.
da:ri cha: shi:shvi ? - Are the windows made of glass?
kamran hInzI cha lAkri hInzI tI kienIci cha shi:shvi - Rooms have wooden, and kitchen has (windows) made of glass.
yi gav ja:n tahi chu panun maka:nI - This is good that you have your own house.
hu lakuT maka:nI kAma sund chu ? - Whose small house is that?
yi chu te:jun - This is Tejaas.
asIl chu. te:ji chu mehanti: - It is fine. Teja is hard working.
a: su chu shari:ph tI ima:nda:r ti - Yes, he is a gentleman and honest as well.

heading: Programme 23
intro: I. In this Programme, we introduce simple verb forms

sections.title: Sample Dialogue
items: I have to go to the garden.
What are you going to do there?
Some labourers are there. They have to bring down (pluck) apples. These are to be packed in wooden boxes and will be sent to market tomorrow.
I too need one or two boxes.
Doesnat matter. Come along with me.
How much is to be paid?
You donat have to pay money. You are my friend.

rows.english: I have to go to the garden.
What are you going to do there?

rows.kashmiri: tati chi k?h mozu:r timan chi tsu:Tha va:lIna . tim chi pe:Taan barIna. paga:h chi manDi: so:zIna
rows.english: Some labourers are there. They have to bring down (pluck) apples. These are to be packed in wooden boxes and will be sent to market tomorrow.

rows.kashmiri: me ti che akh zI peTi zoru:rath
rows.english: I too need one or two boxes.

rows.kashmiri: k?h parva:y chu nI. pAkiv me sI:ta.
rows.english: Doesnat matter. Come along with me.
How much is to be paid?

rows.kashmiri: tahi chivnI pa:sI dina.toha chiv SA:na do:s
rows.english: You donat have to pay money. You are my friend.

sections.title: Listen the names of some fruits and try to remember

heading: Programme 24
intro: I. This Programme introduces the basic verb form used as an imperative.

sections.title: Sample Dialogue
items: Ali, get up now. Have a shower.
He is here. He is watering the flowers in the kitchen garden.
You do one thing. Go to the market and get vegetables.
Get hakh (K) and beans. Get spinach. Also, radish and green chillies as well.
Look for the fish also. Open that almirah and get me my purse.
Take this money. Go fast. Get a newspaper as well.

rows.kashmiri: Ali, tsI vath vana. shra:n kar
rows.english: Ali, get up now. Have a shower.

rows.kashmiri: su chu yet:t: va:ri manz po:shan sag diva:n.
rows.english: He is here. He is watering the flowers in the kitchen garden.

rows.kashmiri: tsI kar kA:m ba:zar gatsh tati an sabzi:
rows.english: You do one thing. Go to the market and get vegetables.

rows.kashmiri: tsI an ha:kh tI ra:zma:h. pa:lakh ti Anazi beyi hezi muji, tI ni:lamartsva:gan.
rows.english: Get hakh (K) and beans. Get spinach. Also, radish and green chillies as well.

rows.kashmiri: beyi vuchtI ga:DI ti. almA:ra kho:l tI mao:n bATwI an yu:ra.
rows.english: Look for the fish also. Open that almirah and get me my purse.

rows.kashmiri: raTh yim rapyi.neI:ir jal. akhba:r ti Anazi
rows.english: Take this money. Go fast. Get a newspaper as well.

heading: Programme 25
intro: I. This lesson introduces you to the main verb and its various forms progressive (present and past). There is no difference in present progressive and present indefinite in Kashmiri.

sections.title: In Kashmiri, the verb bases like par khe ce, etc., are used as the imperatives as well. Some of the verbs used are:
Vocabulary used
items: te:l vor - a type of bakery

sections.title: Sample Dialogue
items: This is my cousin Nanaas letter.
She was studying here previously, but now she is studying medicine in Delhi.
I was taking previously, but now I take milk in the evening with the baked bread (telwor).

rows.kashmiri: yi chi maa:ni paphtIri bene nanIna ciTha
rows.english: This is my cousin Nanaas letter.

rows.kashmiri: saA:s brah yeti: para:n magar vana chasa dili Da:rTari: kara:n
rows.english: She was studying here previously, but now she is studying medicine in Delhi.

rows.kashmiri: bI o:sus brah ceva:n magar vana chus bI sha:man dad ceva:n tI te:lvor kheva:n.
rows.english: I was taking previously, but now I take milk in the evening with the baked bread (telwor).

sections.title: Listen to the modes of address and greeting
items: bADiv bhagva:nas hava:lI - You may leave, God bless you.

heading: Programme 26
intro: I. This Programme introduces you to the future tense simple and progressive. In Kashmiri, future tense is indicated by auxiliary a:si and a:san.

sections.title: The verbs introduced in this conversation are:
Sample Dialogue
items: I think may be on Tuesday.
I am not sure. There is no letter. May be I will get it today.
Daddyas letter. It must be on way, because it is Diwali.
Will you be waiting here tomorrow.
No, I have to go to the market tomorrow.
I will buy goods and vegetables.
Okay, go ahead. God bless you.

rows.english: I think may be on Tuesday.

rows.kashmiri: khabar ciThiy chenI va:ta:n az ma:h va:ti
rows.english: I am not sure. There is no letter. May be I will get it today.

rows.kashmiri: De:Di sI~z ciThahay a:si paka:n dewA:li: chena:
rows.english: Daddyas letter. It must be on way, because it is Diwali.
Will you be waiting here tomorrow.

rows.kashmiri: na, pagah chu me ba:zar gatshun.
rows.english: No, I have to go to the market tomorrow.
I will buy goods and vegetables.
Okay, go ahead. God bless you.

dili pyaThI kar dra:yivI ? - When did you leave Delhi (for here)?
Nazir: - bI dra:s ra:th sha:man , tI az subhan vo:tus
saphras manz thAkava:? - Did you get tired in the travel?
Nazir: - bI thokus nI albatI tAbiyath o:sum nI Thi:kh.
sha gvI na:? - Didnat you sleep?
Nazir: - ahansa: sha gus magar thaka:vaTh tsAj nI
Nazir: - n?dIr a:yi magar kha:s nI k?h
acha: pAkiv garI khasav - Well letas go home.

heading: Programme 29
intro: I. This Programme introduces you to simple past tense with both transitive and intransitive verbs

heading: Programme 30
intro: I. This Programme introduces

sections.title: Sample Dialogue
items: Have you ever been to Kashmir ?
Yes, I had been there last year. I stayed there for a week.
No, my family was with me.
I too had been to Gulmarg last year. I have stayed there overnight. I have also had a trip in the chair lift.
I have also visited Dal lake. I had never enjoyed the Shikara ride.
Yes, in the next month I would have arrived in Kashmir. Come with me.
No. We have our sonas marriage . I would be busy with the wedding ceremony.

rows.english: Have you ever been to Kashmir ?

rows.kashmiri: a: bI o:sus parus to:r go:mut. bI o:sus tati Akis haphtas.
rows.english: Yes, I had been there last year. I stayed there for a week.

rows.kashmiri: na ma:ena garika ti :Asa me sI:ta a:mIta.
rows.english: No, my family was with me.

rows.kashmiri: tsI kath kath ja:yi chukh phau:rmut?
bI ti A:sIs parus gulmarg gA:mIts bI chas tati ra:tas ru:zmIts.tati chas bI ganDo:lahas ti khAtsmIts.
rows.english: I too had been to Gulmarg last year. I have stayed there overnight. I have also had a trip in the chair lift.

rows.kashmiri: bI chus Dalas ti phau:rmut .bI o:sus nI za:h shika:ri khotmut.ja:n mazI a:v .
rows.english: I have also visited Dal lake. I had never enjoyed the Shikara ride.

rows.kashmiri: a: navi retI a:sI bI kAshi:ri vA:tsmIts . pakh tsI ti.
rows.english: Yes, in the next month I would have arrived in Kashmir. Come with me.

rows.kashmiri: na asi chu lADbas kas kha:ndar. bI a:sI timan dohan kha:ndras sI:ta a:vur.
rows.english: No. We have our sonas marriage . I would be busy with the wedding ceremony.

heading: Programme 31
intro: I. This Programme introduces perfective aspect

sections.title: Sample Dialogue
items: Have you seen (attended) a Kashmiri marriage ?
Yeah, I have a friend in Kashmir. He had the wedding during the last year. He had invited me. I was accompanied by my Mummy and Daddy.
Then you would have enjoyed "vazwaan" (a special feast) .
Yes, We have eaten that feast also. They had prepared many good preparations.
Kababs, goshtabs, yakhni, rista and tabakhnat.
I have heard that Kashmiri weddings are worthseeing.
Yes, you have heard right. Where have you seen their Manzira:th. (you havenat seen it.)
Had you taken some present for them.
My Daddy had brought a silver set from Delhi. We gave it to them.
They would have felt very happy.

rows.kashmiri: nasi:ma: tse chutha: kA:shur kha:ndar vuchmut ?
rows.english: Have you seen (attended) a Kashmiri marriage ?

rows.kashmiri: a: me chu kAshI:ri akh do:s . tAmis o:s parus kha:ndar.tAma o:s me sa:las prItshmut.me A:sa mami: DeDi: ti si:ta.
rows.english: Yeah, I have a friend in Kashmir. He had the wedding during the last year. He had invited me. I was accompanied by my Mummy and Daddy.
Then you would have enjoyed "vazwaan" (a special feast) .

rows.kashmiri: a: asi chu khaomut.timav a:sI va:rya:h ziya:phtsi ranimatsI.
rows.english: Yes, We have eaten that feast also. They had prepared many good preparations.

rows.kashmiri: kaba:b, gashta:b, yekhIna, ristI tI tabakhna:Th .
rows.english: Kababs, goshtabs, yakhni, rista and tabakhnat.

rows.kashmiri: me chu bu:zmut, kA:shur kha:ndar chu vuchun la:yakh a:sa:n
rows.english: I have heard that Kashmiri weddings are worthseeing.

rows.kashmiri: a: chu th Thi:kh bu:zmut.tse kati chath tihInz mA:zira:th vuchhmIts.
rows.english: Yes, you have heard right. Where have you seen their Manzira:th. (you havenat seen it.)

rows.kashmiri: tahi o:sva: timan k?h prazant nyumut ?
rows.english: Had you taken some present for them.

rows.kashmiri: a: me:na DeDiyan o:s dili ca:ndi seT haotmut.su daut asi timan.
rows.english: My Daddy had brought a silver set from Delhi. We gave it to them.
They would have felt very happy.

sections.title: Listen and remember the body parts

heading: Programme 32
intro: I. This Programme introduces

sections.title: Let us listen to the conversation on abaDshaha - aA famous King of Kashmira
items: tse chutha baDsha:has mutlak bu:zmut ? - Have you heard about Badshah ?

a: su chu mAshu:r ba:dsah o:smut dapa:n tAma karnA:v kAsi:r seTha: tarki: magar me chanI zya:dI pata: - Yes. He was the famous king. It is said that Kashmir made enough progress under his rule. But I do not know much, let you tell me.
tAmakhanIna:vi nAhri.tema bana:vna:vi saDkI .tAma dya:vnA:vavatan kula. - He got the canals dug. He got the roads constructed. He got the trees planted on the road sides.
beyi kaa: kaa: kor tAma ? - What else did he (got done) do ?
TAma bula:vnA:vanebIrI pyeThI kA:ragar. Yimav yetika lu:kh va:rya: kasIb hechinA:va. - He invited the artisans from other parts, who trained the locals in various skills.
Dapa:n tAma sIndi vakhatI o:s kA shi:ri manz miltsa:r - It is said that during hisreign there was brotherhood among the Kashmiris.

a: tami bro:hA:sasultan sikandaran kA:sira pAnDith kAshi:ri manzI tsalnA:vimita. baDsha:han anInA:vatim va:pas . - Yes, before his reign Sikandar Sultan had shunted out the Kashmiri Pundits from Kashmir. Badshah arranged their return.
tAma cha kA:shir zaba:n ti tarki: karnA :vmIts. - He got the Kashmiri language also developed.

a: tAma pak no:v kA:shur ko:m prath rangI bro:nh .su o:s A:liman kadIr kara:n tI timan khya:van tI cha:van .tAma lekhno:v Sri Bhat ja: tava:ribh su o:s ja:yi ja:yi dra:ma tava:rikh s u o:s gindna:va:n - Yes, he got the Kashmiri community developed in various ways. He used to honour the scholars and treat them well. He got Sri Bhat to pen down the history of Kashmir. He used to get the plays performed.

sections.title: Listen and repeat 1. tAma karnA:v chuTi 2. me bo:znA:va sA:riy kath 3. sarka:ran bana:vi na:vi saDk 4. timav khanInA:va khoD 5. me bula:vnA:va lADkI 6. sikandaran A:sa baTI tsalnA:v mit 7. su chu tAmis batI khya:va:n 8. tAma le:khno:v akh maka:lI 9. tim chi shuren gindIna:va:n 10. bI chus kA:shur parnava:n IV. Listen and remember the flowers

heading: Programme 33
intro: I. This Programme introduces you to the modal verbs in Kashmiri. The modal verbs in Kashmiri are:

sections.title: This Programme introduces you to the modal verbs in Kashmiri. The modal verbs in Kashmiri are:
Sample Dialogue
items: No, I do not know driving.
That is right, I should learn driving now.
I know driving but I wonat be in a position to drive today. My foot is injured.
Are you intending to go to city today?
Come on Let us go in a bus, we can return by evening.

rows.kashmiri: na, me chanI taga:n gA:Da cala:vIna.
rows.english: No, I do not know driving.

rows.kashmiri: ti chu Thi:kh , me va:ti vona gA:D: cala:vIna hechina.
rows.english: That is right, I should learn driving now.

rows.kashmiri: me cha taga:n gA:Da cala:vIna magar me bani nI az cala:vIna. me chu khoras logmut
rows.english: I know driving but I wonat be in a position to drive today. My foot is injured.

rows.kashmiri: tsI chukha yatsha:n az shahar gatshun ?
rows.english: Are you intending to go to city today?

rows.kashmiri: pakh, basi maz gatshav, sha:man ta:m hyakav va:pas yith
rows.english: Come on Let us go in a bus, we can return by evening.

sections.title: The modal verb forms used in the conversation are:

heading: Programme 34
intro: I. This Programme introduces adverbials

sections.title: Sample Dialogue
items: Come in, Mr.Ali, sit down here. What brings you here today?
I was just passing hereby. I thought I should come here. Do you sit in this room.
Yes, I and Mr. Rehman sit in this room.
The boss sits in that room.
This room is small. Do you find it enough ? That room is big. Why donat you sit there ?
No that room does not have enough light. That room is at the back side. This room is on the front.
Doesnat that room have a window?
Yes, that has one window, but that is small.
This room has big windows. It should be allowing in enough air.
Yes, one can see directly at the bossas room from here.
Is it not far away from there.
No, the boss also stays there. He shuttles there in his car. I come with him only. That is why it does not look far away.
That is fine. God bless you.

rows.kashmiri: vAliv Atsiv, ali sA:b bihiv yeten. az kith pA:Tha yot ?
rows.english: Come in, Mr.Ali, sit down here. What brings you here today?

rows.kashmiri: bas yapA:ra o:sus paka:n me su:ch yo:r ti yimI toha chiva: yeth kamras manz beha:n ?
rows.english: I was just passing hereby. I thought I should come here. Do you sit in this room.

rows.kashmiri: a: yeth kamras manz chi bI tI Rahma:n sA:b beha:n
rows.english: Yes, I and Mr. Rehman sit in this room.

rows.kashmiri: saha:b chu hath kamras maz a:sa:n
rows.english: The boss sits in that room.

rows.kashmiri: yi kamrI chu lakuT. toha chiva: yeti va:ta:n.hu kamrI chu boD.toha kaa:zi chiv nI tati beha:n.
rows.english: This room is small. Do you find it enough ? That room is big. Why donat you sit there ?

rows.kashmiri: na tati chu nI ga:sh .su chu na: pathkun.yi kamrI chu bra:Thkun .
rows.english: No that room does not have enough light. That room is at the back side. This room is on the front.
Doesnat that room have a window?

rows.kashmiri: a: tath cha akh dA:r magar s cha lalIT
rows.english: Yes, that has one window, but that is small.

rows.kashmiri: yeth kamras cha baji da:ri. yepA:ra a:si ja:n hava: ti yiva:n.
rows.english: This room has big windows. It should be allowing in enough air.

rows.kashmiri: a: yeti peThI cha sahab sIndis kamaras kun ti nazar
rows.english: Yes, one can see directly at the bossas room from here.

rows.kashmiri: Tati peThI ma: chu du:r gatsha:n.
rows.english: Is it not far away from there.

rows.kashmiri: na sahab ti chu tati ro:za:n su chu to:r ka:rimanz yiva:n gatsha:n .bI chu tAsa sI:ta yiva:n tamikin, chu nI du:r ba:sa:n
rows.english: No, the boss also stays there. He shuttles there in his car. I come with him only. That is why it does not look far away.

rows.kashmiri: ti gav ja:n ad sA: tI las
rows.english: That is fine. God bless you.

sections.title: Listen and repeat 1. yeti cha tI:r 2. yot a:v agre:z 3. yo:r chi shi:nI ambar 4. hoten chu jalsI 5. hot ti chu gatshun 6. hoti paav shi:n 7. tati A:s tI:r 8. tot gAyi sA:ri: lu:kh 9. to:r o:s po:ni: po:na IV. Listen and repeat some more adverbial forms

heading: Programme 35
intro: I. This Programme introduces to the comparatives and reduplicatives in Kashmiri

sections.title: This Programme introduces to the comparatives and reduplicatives in Kashmiri
Sample Dialogue
items: A: mohna:tsI kar a:kh kAshi:ri peThI ? - Mohan, when did you return from Kashmir ?
M: bI ha: a:s u:trI - I came day before yesterday
A: tati kyuth mosam chu az kal ? - How about the climate there ?
M: tati cha yeti ki kho:tI tI:rIy paham. - It is a bit colder there than here.
A: yeti chi tati ki khotI hame:shI gi a:sa:n - It is always hotter here than there.
M: valsA:va:rI va:rI vati yeti ti tI:r . vadI a:v na? - Okay gradually it will be cold here also. Winter has set in ?
A: to:ti chu roza:n tati ki khotI vushne:r - Even then it is hotter here in comparision to that place.
M: hayo: tse vuchthana: mushta:k? - Hey, did you see Mushtaq ?
A: ahunu: me samkhaav ra:th - Yes, I met him yesterday.
M: kaah o:s kara:n ? - What was he doing ?
A: su o:s Ti:vi: vucha:n - He was watching TV.
M: katha: ba:tha: kArvah ? - Did you have a chat ?
A: kathI kara:n kara:n gAyi ra:th - We passed the night chatting.
M: acha pAkiv ne:rav - Okay, let us go.
A: bI thokus pAka pAka basi khasav - I feel tired of walking. Let us board a bus.
M: adI teli khasav basi - Okay, let us board a bus.

sections.title: The comparatives and reduplicated forms in the conversation are:

Listen and repeat 1. mohanI chu mya:ni khotI zau:Th 2. shi:lI cha ka:tIni kho:tI zi:Th 3. lAkI chi koraan hIndi kho:tI moTa 4. kAshi:ri cha Dili hIndi kho:tI zaa:dI tI:r a:sa:n 5. pATya:la: chu je:mi khotI du:r 6. hos chu va:rI va:rI paka:n 7. sIh chu te:z te:z do:ra:n 8. ko:ri cha kA:m kara:n kara:n re:Dyo: bo:za:n 9. bI chus shra:n kara:n kara:n gaava:n V. Listen and remember the vocabules

heading: Programme 36
intro: I. This Programme introduces passive constructions

sections.title: Listen and repeat 1. he:rath cha vandas manz mana:vnI yiva:n 2. shivra:tri chu kA:shira pA:Tha he:rath vanInI yiva:n 3. maka:nI chi livni yiva:n 4. maka:nan chu DuvnI yiva:n 5. vaTukh chu anInI yiva:n 6. pu:zah cha karnI yiva:n 7. pAtshis chu khA:tir karnI yiva:n 8. damo:lu: chi ranInI yiva:n 9. taATh sabzi chanI khenI yiva:n IV. Listen and memorize the following Kashmiri dishes:
items: nadIra yekhIn - dish of lotus roots

heading: Programme 37
intro: I. This Programme introduces apronominalizationa phenomenon associated with the verb. The verbs included are:

sections.title: This Programme introduces apronominalizationa phenomenon associated with the verb. The verbs included are:
items: gatsho:vI - I will go to you
so:zas - I will send him/ her

heading: Programme 38
intro: I. This Programme introduces you to the conditional constructions.

sections.title: Sample Dialogue
items: S: Aliya tsI gatsh kha: yuhus kAshi:ri - Ali would you go to Kashmir this year?
A: agar chuTi: mi:jim, teli gatshI - In case I get leave I shall go.
S: yuhus chu na: rAshi:das kha:ndar ? - It is Rashidas marriage this year.
A: agar tAmis no:kri: me:Ii teli kari su kha:ndar - In case he gets employment then he will marry.
S: tAmsIndis kha:ndras peyi:y tse gatshun - You shall have to attend his marriage.
A: yeli su kari , teli bo:zav - It will be decided when he marries.
S: yuhus kauth garIm ro:zi ju:nas manz ? - How hot it would be in June this year?
A: agar ru:d pev, teli ro:zi mo:sam khoshgava:r . - If it rains then it would be pleasant.
S: bI chas sa:ca:n ju:n guza:rIha: yuhus kA shi:ri: - This year I am planning to spend June in Kashmir.
A: seTha: ja:n gav. - That would be fine.

sections.title: Listen and repeat 1. agar ru:d lo:gun, pakun gatshi mushkil 2. yeli su yiyi ,teli vuchav 3. agar po:na sa:ph a:sihe, teli karihe su shra:n 4. yeli su phe:l gav, teli a:yas akIl 5. agar tAmis shura chi, teli cha sakha:ndar kArmIts 6. yeli tAmis shur za:v, teli gayi tAmis hash khash. IV. Listen to the numerals and memorize
